focusPoints.label: What “good” looks like right now.
focusPoints.reason: Whether his expectations feel clear after ~three months, especially with shifting requirements—worth clarifying what feels fuzzy and what he’s using as his bar for success.

focusPoints.label: How safe he feels asking for help.
focusPoints.reason: If he’s carrying frustration, one possibility is he’s not feeling fully safe to ask early; worth exploring what feels hardest to raise and what would make it easier.

focusPoints.label: Support he needs from you this week.
focusPoints.reason: If the role feels harder than it needs to be, a junior’s biggest unlock in a “something feels off” 1:1 is concrete manager support—what to unblock, what decisions to make, and how you can reduce the uncertainty loop.

focusPoints.label: What he’s saying yes and no to.
focusPoints.reason: Whether shifting requirements are pulling him into rework or thrash, it helps to map what he’s currently prioritizing and what he’s intentionally deprioritizing so he can build confidence through clearer sequencing.

name: Kenji
notes: Kenji is around three months in, and this is a common point where uncertainty and confidence dips show up. I need to understand whether he feels clear on expectations, connected to support, and safe asking for help. Notes suggest he may be carrying frustration about shifting requirements while trying to prove himself. This should stay exploratory, not corrective: I want to hear what feels hardest right now and where our team setup is making the role harder than it needs to be.
